title: cheirinia odorata
intro: Cheirinia odorata, also known as the wild jasmine or jasmine of the rocks, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. Cheirinia odorata has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and supporting the immune system. Cheirinia odorata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to promote relaxation and well-being.
